lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Alexandria. The Mouseion still stands — but its silence is the silence of a budget line that has simply been struck out.

lines.speaker: Hypatia
lines.text: People speak of fire. Fire is dramatic. Fire is blameless. The truth is less poetic: Rome stopped paying.

lines.speaker: Hypatia
lines.text: Salaries. Grain rations for resident scholars. The stipends that let a man spend a decade copying one text without starving. When those stopped, the scholars left. And when scholars leave, they carry what they know — and what they can carry.

lines.speaker: Mele
lines.text: So the exodus was the preservation.

lines.speaker: Hypatia
lines.text: Exactly. The Empire meant to let the collection quietly wither. Instead it scattered the texts across every city that would take a learned refugee. Rome's neglect became the first diaspora of knowledge.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The first strategy was not a strategy at all. It was survival. And survival, it turned out, was enough.
